

Saigon   
Equities   
Forum

## **Introduction**

**Saigon Equities Forum (SEF)** is a student-led financial research organization with a focus on Vietnam. We produce research reports and insights on the country’s macroeconomy and capital markets, including listed companies and industries on the VN-Index.

Our goal is to create an academic platform where ideas on Vietnam’s capital markets can be debated, research is accessible and shared freely, and the next generation of thinkers can engage in reshaping their community.

![][image1]

## **Our Story**

SEF was founded by two friends, Nam Nguyen and Hung Tran, on the wish that there should be endless opportunities to learn finance. We maintain a set of core values meant to be as open as possible to aspiring finance professionals looking for a way to explore capital markets via our emerging economy.

## **Core Values**

**Integrity**

- We strive to be honest, unbiased, and transparent with our research ethics, upholding the highest standards of academic excellence. Our work remains independent and free from vested interests.

**Collaboration**

- We strive to bring together voices across multiple disciplines, fostering constructive dialogue and ensuring well-informed and well-rounded perspectives in our work.

**Public Service**

- We work with the community in mind—promoting financial literacy, sharing knowledge openly, and ensuring that our research serves the broader public good.

## **Equity Research Analyst**

SEF’s Equity Research team stands at the forefront of our mission on developing and producing comprehensive reports on Vietnamese equities that are listed on all three HoSE, HNX and UpCom exchanges. Our reports are vetted by leadership, senior advisors in Vietnamese finance, and are used by a large audience including current students and working professionals. Our coverage includes: Technology, Media, Telecoms; Consumer Retail; Healthcare; Energy; Banking.

**Responsibilities**

- Conduct primary and secondary research including industry reports, HOSE/HNX disclosures, sector developments, macroeconomic data to develop differentiated investment theses  
- Build and maintain three-statement financial models from company filings, earnings releases, news articles  
- Apply valuation frameworks such as Comparable Analysis, Discounted Cash Flow, Dividend Discount Model, Sum-of-the-Parts, etc.  
- Lead coverage groups, author initiation-of-coverage reports and periodic updates with a clear Overweight/Underweight recommendation and 6-month price target  
- Present research findings to SEF's editorial board and broader membership in a clear, structured format  
- Collaborate with the Macroeconomics team to incorporate top-down views into sector and company-level analysis  
- Maintain data integrity across financial models, ensuring consistency with source filings  
- Experiment with and incorporate AI tools to streamline research and analysis process

**You will need**

- Currently enrolled in Finance, Economics, Business Administration or a related field  
  - If in high school, be Junior standing  
- Demonstrated interest in equity research, asset management, or investment banking through coursework, clubs, or prior experience  
  - If no prior experience, demonstrate clear intent to learn  
- Proficiency in Excel, Word, and PowerPoint; experience building financial models from scratch is a strong plus  
- Familiarity in Vietnamese Accounting Standards  
- Ironclad written and communication skills in English; Vietnamese proficiency is not required but preferred due to research sources being in Vietnamese  
- Ability to manage deadlines independently and deliver polished work with minimal supervision

**You will gain**

- Hands-on experience producing institutional-quality equity research on Vietnamese public companies, peer equities, and American equities  
- A published research portfolio directly relevant to buy-side and sell-side recruiting  
- Network access to SEF's peers in finance across Vietnam, Europe, and the US  
- Access to in-house development programs, including access to FinancialEdge, TrainingTheStreet, BIWS  
- Meritocratic working environment, with close group of peers striving to work as hard as possible

**Tools Required**

- MS Office, CafeF, FactSet, LSEG Workspace, CapitalIQ, Figma.   
  - *We will try to provide access if you do not have any of the above, however it is not guaranteed*

**Time commitment:** 

- \~4-6 hours/week

To streamline the recruitment process, we will only have 2 rounds. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews.

Please apply via our Google Forms link: [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1\_yQ9IyP0JLHexHRUwk9CzELAujD9k7Qxm0si3\_kCm8g/edit](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1_yQ9IyP0JLHexHRUwk9CzELAujD9k7Qxm0si3_kCm8g/edit) 

## 

## **Macro Research Analyst**

The Macroeconomics team strives to link academic research and practical financial applications at the highest levels. We take macroeconomic indicators and policy developments relevant to Vietnam and regional peers, including updates from SBV, NSO, ADB, World Bank, etc. to produce investor-positive trends and insights within domestic industries of our capital market. Our reports are vetted by leadership, senior advisors in Vietnamese finance, and are used by a large audience including current students and working professionals. 

**Responsibilities**

- Conduct primary and secondary research including industry reports, policy, economic data updates, regulatory developments from the SBV, GSO, and relevant government bodies on a rolling basis, to develop differentiated investment theses  
- Assess the capital market implications of policy shifts, including rate decisions, credit ceiling adjustments, and fiscal stimulus measures, as well as cover GDP, inflation, credit growth, trade balance, FDI flows, exchange rate dynamics, urbanization, manufacturing FDI rotation, and regional trade dynamics to produce clear takeaways for equity and fixed income investors  
- Incorporate theoretical research data and quantitative methods from academic papers into practical usage  
- Track regional peer economies (Thailand, Indonesia, Philippines, Malaysia) to benchmark Vietnam's macro trajectory  
- Collaborate with SEF's Equity Research team to incorporate macro views into sector and company-level coverage  
- Present findings to SEF membership through written reports and editorial contributions  
- Experiment with and incorporate AI tools to streamline research and analysis process


**You will need**

- Currently enrolled in Economics, International Relations, Public Policy, Finance, or a related field  
  - If in high school, be Junior standing  
- Strong understanding of macroeconomics and familiarity with Vietnamese economic institutions and policy infrastructure  
- Demonstrated interest in economics research, quantitative research, asset management, or investment banking through coursework, clubs, or prior experience  
  - If no prior experience, demonstrate clear intent to learn  
- Proficiency in Excel, Word, and PowerPoint; experience compacting large volumes of information into clear, concise, and well-structured infographics  
- Ironclad written and communication skills in English; Vietnamese proficiency is not required but preferred due to research sources being in Vietnamese  
- Ability to manage deadlines independently and deliver polished work with minimal supervision

**You will gain**

- Hands-on experience producing institutional-quality macro research on Vietnamese public companies, peer equities, and American equities  
- A published research portfolio  covering one of Southeast Asia's most dynamic emerging market economies  
- Network access to SEF's peers in finance across Vietnam, Europe, and the US  
- Access to in-house development programs, including access to FinancialEdge, TrainingTheStreet, BIWS  
- Meritocratic working environment, with close group of peers striving to work as hard as possible

**Tools Required**

- MS Office, CafeF, FactSet, LSEG Workspace, CapitalIQ, Figma.   
  - *We will try to provide access if you do not have any of the above, however it is not guaranteed*

**Time commitment:** 

- \~4-6 hours/week

To streamline the recruitment process, we will only have 2 rounds. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews.

Please apply via our Google Forms link: [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1\_yQ9IyP0JLHexHRUwk9CzELAujD9k7Qxm0si3\_kCm8g/edit](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1_yQ9IyP0JLHexHRUwk9CzELAujD9k7Qxm0si3_kCm8g/edit) 

## **Head of Partnerships**

SEF's Head of Partnerships owns our external relationships and public affairs. They build and maintain a live network of working professionals across finance, academic institutions, and peer organizations from Vietnam to Europe and America, that translates directly into programming, opportunity, and long-term value for SEF members and Vietnamese academia. 

**Responsibilities**

- Identify, cold-reach, and build lasting relationships with finance professionals across Vietnam, Southeast Asia, Europe, Australia, and the US including investment banking, asset management, private equity, and corporate finance  
- Maintain a structured contact pipeline, ensuring regular touchpoints with existing partners and a steady flow of new outreach  
- Develop and execute networking events, panels, coffee chats, fireside sessions that connect SEF members with peer clubs and practitioners in target fields  
- Build institutional relationships with universities, high schools, and student finance organizations in Vietnam and abroad to establish formal club partnerships and reciprocal programming  
- Translate partnership pipeline into measurable outcomes: events run, professionals engaged, member placements facilitated, and institutional MOUs signed  
- Work closely with SEF's research and editorial teams to position external-facing content as a credibility asset in outreach

**What you will need**

- Currently enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate program; background in Finance, Economics, Business, or Communications preferred but not required  
  - If in high school, be Junior standing  
- Demonstrated comfort with cold outreach and relationship cultivation — you don't wait to be introduced  
- Ironclad written and communication skills in English and Vietnamese  
- Commercially minded to think about relationships in terms of what they can unlock, and you know how to make the ask  
- Organized and systematic: able to manage a large contact book, track follow-ups, and keep the pipeline moving without dropping threads  
- Prior experience in sales, business development, recruiting, or student organization leadership is a strong plus  
- Though position is remote, ability to be present in Vietnam a strong plus

**What You'll Gain**

- A live, senior professional network built during your educational years, across Vietnamese finance, SEA capital markets, and Wall Street  
- Direct experience in institutional relationship-building and partnership development, directly transferable to any front-office careers  
- Ownership of a high-visibility function within one of Vietnam's most active student finance organizations  
- Meritocratic working environment, with close group of peers striving to work as hard as possible

**Time commitment:** 

- \~4-6 hours/week

To streamline the recruitment process, we will only have 2 rounds. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews.

Please apply via our Google Forms link: [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1\_yQ9IyP0JLHexHRUwk9CzELAujD9k7Qxm0si3\_kCm8g/edit](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1_yQ9IyP0JLHexHRUwk9CzELAujD9k7Qxm0si3_kCm8g/edit)
